entities.name: HUNGER
entities.description: A state of suffering or disease caused by lack of food

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The state of being extremely poor

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The artificial application of water to land or soil

entities.name: DRAINAGE
entities.description: The natural or artificial removal of surface and sub-surface water from an area

entities.name: WATER SCARCITY
entities.description: The lack of sufficient available water resources to meet demands

entities.name: MILLENNIUM DEVELOPMENT GOALS
entities.description: International development goals established to eliminate hunger and poverty

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS
entities.description: Global goals designed to achieve a more sustainable future and eliminate hunger and poverty

entities.name: UNITED NATIONS
entities.description: International organization that initiates development frameworks

entities.name: GOVERNANCE
entities.description: The system by which irrigation and drainage are controlled and operated

entities.name: RIGHTS-BASED DEVELOPMENTS
entities.description: Development approaches based on the realization of human rights

entities.name: WATER RIGHTS
entities.description: Legal rights to use water from a particular source

entities.name: PRICING
entities.description: The determination of the cost of water resources

entities.name: MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of controlling water resources and irrigation systems

entities.name: EFFICIENCY IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of increasing the ratio of output to input in irrigation

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Innovative tools and solutions used to cater to irrigation challenges

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The effectiveness of agricultural output

entities.name: EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The state of achieving maximum productivity with minimum wasted effort or expense

entities.name: WATER LOSSES
entities.description: The loss of water from a system through leakage or evaporation

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: Increasing agricultural yields without adverse environmental impact

entities.name: WATER RESOURCES
entities.description: Sources of water that are useful or potentially useful to humans
